reasoning: Turks and Caicos Islands has a population of ~45,000. The current 360-day baseline average is 1.000, confirming exactly 0 protests in the past year. Thus, the target of "more than ten times" the baseline translates to 11 or more distinct ACLED 'Protest' events strictly within each 30-day window. There is no news of any civil unrest, strikes, or protests currently ongoing in the territory; in fact, a recent election in February 2025 concluded smoothly with a landslide victory for the incumbent party. Given the small population and complete lack of recent civil unrest history, it is incredibly unlikely that 11 or more distinct protest events would suddenly occur (and be logged by ACLED) over any 30-day period. Because this requires an unprecedented black-swan crisis, the probabilities are set at or near the lowest allowed boundary, with very minor increases for the later dates due to time-horizon uncertainty.
